column4: Is there anything you would like to tell us about your experience?
QA testing for the first time form went live
Would be useful to be able to easily access TPO guidance once you are in the application process.
Nominee e mail was sent to me and NOT to the nominee!
Not easy to use a follow
A relatively easy to use.

I couldn't submit my application yesterday as the website was down for maintenance. Why would you choose to do maintenance at 6pm when users are likely to still be working and needing to submit applications. Why not do the maintenance overnight or at weekends. Also if you are doing updates it would be useful if you could email all users so that we are aware. A banner on the website is not useful if you are not checking the website every day.

It is very frustrating when uploading a document that contains a (,) or other non accepted symbols. If one is added with a symbol among multiple other documents that are okay, you cant just remove the one offending document, all have to be removed and then the process has to happen all over again. This can be quite annoying when you have spent 10 minutes or more labelling the files and giving descriptions - and then learn that you cant upload them because of one comma.
Good website!
The graphic arrangement of the form is confusing and unclear.

Generally all works well. But one gripe is adding plans and documents in bulk - if one file contains a character not allowed you cannot just remove that file. i.e. you have completed all types of documents, file names etc. then upload, and it says invalid filename, you have to remove them all and start again, rather than just remove the file with the issue.

thanks
More difficult than it needs to be. The council already knows where trees are in people's gardens so why do we need to do plans/ sketches!

We missed two addresses of our first attempt. Our fault. However.
Please introduce a warning.
It cost us £325.00.

documets required -  do not match the requirements of the local councils also unable to upload bng metrics. domestic solar related applications only avalable on the odd council and calculating the fee point
There is always a lag in being able to download the application submitted. Often you have to come in and out of the application multiple times before it is available to download.
really easy to use
REALLY GOOD AND EASY TO USE

It is really frustrating when you upload multiple documents, select document types for all of them but then find an error message at the bottom of the page saying one of the files cannot be accepted because of a character such as '&' in the file name. You can't just remove this file and try again, you have to repeat the whole process! Hugely frustrating and a waste of time. Plus, if you select a document type that is not 'Other' you should not have to enter a description - some of the options such as 'Section' and 'Planning Statement' are self-explanatory but we are still required to enter a description. Just annoying little bugs that could and should have been picked up in testing!

You need to update your photo image allowance to permit the new format for iphones which is a  HEIC file format.

Otherwise excellent
Using first time was fairly easy to navigate.

I was unable to find a way to save the form and complete it at a later stage.
Stephanie Siroto
Not enough choice with regard to Certificates of Lawful use in relation to operational development
Its not really clear that documents provided have to fit the titles of the 3 sections in that part of the application.
occasional error pops up requiring me to go back to the homescreen

I’m not sure if I’ve done it all right. I was asked to provide a sketch. I can’t draw. I’d rather a real person just came out and we did an application together because there’s loads of points I wish to make but it’s so hard to do so online, and I have many photos but with two small children it’s near impossible to go through a lengthy application process. I hope my experience going forward is much better with me being a bit lame with technology. 

Many thanks.

Things to improve:
- When uploading multiple files, allow a single file to be removed if too large of an incompatible file name. currently, all files have to be uploaded again.

- BNG Xcel metric worksheets never upload  

Thank you
The application form is very simple to complete and is easily manoeuvrable when looking for information elsewhere in the form
BNG compliance for external works to a block of flats was a nightmare to achieve & client thought it was a scam.

The portal is generally straight forward to use however we have recently experienced problems when uploading documents.  File names with permitted characters are frequently rejected as being unsuitable for upload.  More information is required please.
The portal's getting better. Just need to streamline the quantity of unnecessary reports required for simple applications and you'll be golden.

The website worked well but it was not clear what a small domestic solar panel installation is classed as for a certificate of lawful development.  It would assist with clarity, for the average non-specialist who is trying to be a good citizen and doing their own work to keep the costs down, to add this as an option for the type of development.
despite of charging £85 for the service fees, you dont provide map for prior approval applications!!!!

Navigating the main parts of the tool was relatively easy once you know how, but I found the entire experience quite stressful. There are certain parts where it doesnt let you proceed any further even when there is nothing more to add. And the fact you have changed your bank account details recently meant my bank thought it was a scam so i had to use my credit card to pay for myapplication to provide some security
I contacted one of your advisors re: disabled exemption fees and he was very helpful and knowledgeable, thank you.
excellent
So simple and faster than before i love it !!!
- please add clients name and email automatically for fees payment nomination screen, this was previously there but for some reason in new update was removed.
Very easy to use

Simple and easy to use. I have been using the PP since the beginning and never had any issues.

My only snag is when adding materials you need to scroll back up the page the enter details. Does not flow very well.
The planning team has been very helpful thank you

The system worked well once I was using it, however you help page did not indicate how you make a Non Material change application. It does not say use the planning portal and select Non Material application. Which is obvious once you know but your help page should state that the Planning portal is the method of applying for the application. Put simply the help page tells you what you need to do but not what to use to do it.

I found it difficult to navigate at the beginning. Moreover when I wanted to deal with my own tree I had to first wade through lots of other items. Perhaps it’s me as I am a technophobe. Generals I find that those who set up these websites have little empathy for Joe Soap.
Need facility to update name on declaration, does not seem to pull through after updating name in profile.
Very easy to use, intuitive, information is helpful,
I wanted to add photographs of the tree to be removed but could not work out how to choose a document type when I tried to add photographs
First time having to do the application it was difficult but I was able to get through.

I found it really hard navigating around the design and application.
I just thik sometimes a short phone call would advise me how to proceed
Struggled with site plans, especially as laptop or proper computer needed for some types of plans.
fandadbydosey

Overall the process is great. It is a bit of a problem when I try to upload a site boundary and it won't allow it on a mobile device (my mobile is far better than my old laptop) other than that I think the website is good.
Much better way of payment screen now, however your processing charge of £85 is a bit steep. ( I remember when it as £35.00)
Having created a copier of a previous application and given the copy a new name, it would have been handy to have an opportunity to go back and amend that chosen application name.

The fee for this service is increasing every year, and most of the time I receive notification of invalidity because some additional documents should have been submitted with the application; in some case I have to pay an extra admin fee of £ 60.00 for this reason.
I wonder what is the point of paying you if you provide incomplete information.
Everything seemed quite straight forward
too much internal planning/council jargon that is not explained.
Not that easy!

I found the process drawing and feel I have spent 4 hours completing this process - there must be a better way than using the portal.  Word count re description not correct even although I checked this, not able to upload additional cad drawings with the excuse the file name not correct yet I deleted, changed and switched around - still no good.  It is not work the £85.00 to use and feel it is outrageous to for BCC to ask the public to go through this process.
Pretty good process for the application - hope it comes back with a result!
seems to be straight forward
straight forward and easy to use
Generally straightforward
When you are only applying to drop a kerb the form is complicated and totally over the top
Forms were not able to be downloaded and completed. Very clunky system not straight forward
Intuitive and easy to use
Very well designed and easy to use tool
There is a degree of repetition in listing the drawings and their titles, but I suppose it is the only way to deal with them all!

There must be a shorter way to do this for small projects like solar panels, a garden wall, erecting a flagpole etc.
Some of this is bureaucracy gone made but apart from that its easy to use.
Why should I pay a service fee for submitting a planning application when there is already a fee for the planning application - clearly the fee is for processing the application!
So much better than it used to be, although the auto fill for agent section quite often overwrites the clients details with the agents.
Very easy to complete.
There seems to be repetition that can be avoided, by having categorised consents
one or two of the questions are a little vague and need a little more explanation.
Brillient service
Site plan tool to be made available for free site plans at this fee
No, it all seems to be working well since the portal was upgraded.  No issues.

You need to change the documents submission page - if you submit one document which has a comma, or & or such like in, you need to cancel the whole lot and start again.  you need to change it so you can replace the document with another, or simply delete that document, but keep all the others.  its super frustrating to have added say 20 documents in bulk, named most of them, to find one with an error in the name and start again from scratch.
Frictionless!
great phone advice, and simple to follow online instructions.

repeated pop ups about payment despite ticking the box (about 20 times) saying don't show me this again 
Failing to upload 19 documents because one has an errant comma in it so all have to be removed and resubmitted
the location plan drawing tool being basically useless for anything other than standard house in a town on a street and continuation to charge an 'admin fee' for zero admin.
good portal
not the easiest experience for a couple of pensioners to use although we are quite computer savvy.
too expensive - there should be a reduction in cost if we have already paid for the mapping on a previous application for the same site.
Quick and Easy

I had to go to Uttlesford District Council offices today because I didn't know that sending in documents to fulfill conditions needed a 'new' application.  I had to spend a lot of time trying to find out how I was supposed to submit the documents I had prepared.
I could not upload any documents to the mandatory section, everything went to the additional information
no
Site plan was difficult to navigate
Overeall Good, But i cant reach Planning team via Phone or email, so many versions are going. That should reduce to help customers more. thanks.
easy peasy just pricey!!!!
I was unable top upload the Statutory Biodiversity Metric
You put the service charge up but do nothing to make the system better!!!!
Pretty logical ans nice to use!
Good and relatively easy to use
Very easy to complete the application
Crashed lost everything
Still clunky and strongly biased towards the authorities time rather than helping my firm to progress the system...could do better and be more concerned with the agents perspective
Works well
Very user friendly
There was an issue with Payment transaction.
Very straightforward, thank you. The pdf titles with dashes and characters in can be a nuisance as they have to be re-titled but otherwise everything is great:-)
It took many attempts on this occasion to upload documents

Had to resubmit as the house and garden of the property are in different authorities. no way to just send on to the correct council despite it costing nearly £100 for you to forward the information.  
The planning portal has become to expensive.

It's not clear when trying to ascertain which planning application is required re: running a cable over the pavement. You need to provide a FAQ with detail on which planning application is required for such items as mentioned above.

all good,so far
the portal fee seems a bit high
The portal is very good.  Easy to use and comprehensive.
Had the wrong name in my profile. I could not change the name (for the signature) in the application even though I had changed it in my profile :(
I have found it very difficult to attach documents to my application. I have emailed the necessary documents and hope this is acceptable
Great!
please could you return the nominated payer suggestion

Please change the need to add the nominee details manually. It was better before. If this can be added automatically, then this should be fantastic. It will save not only time, but also frustration to have to go back to the our system to find the client details
It's a very simple, straight forward process. Thank you for simplifying the process.

Occasionally the LPA will retain a fee from an earlier withdrawn application in order to funfdn a similar resubmission. There appera to be no provision for this circumstance when (re)submitting an appliation via the Planning Portal.
Can be a bit laggy when swapping between sections of the application form and especially when saving and closing
Drawing tool for drawing boundary is very difficult to use
easy to understand and fill details

Portal would not let me create location plan again, said that I was not using a desktop computer (which I am) tried using different isp's microsoft edge and google chrome and still didn't work. Same problem that occurred about a month or so ago which was cured but now has recurred.
I didn’t find the application procedure very intuitive. I couldn’t download the planning portal map on my ipad.
The location plan tool is still sub-par. It works brilliantly for a small site, but is rubbish for a large site......very unwieldy where one is having to draw freehand.
It was not easy to locate the section 73 from the outset. Once found the process was good.

There were parts that did not relate to my application but I had to fill them in or provide plans (eg. my application was for a handrail at the side of my steps but it asked for a waste management documentation)
The help I received today from the helpdesk was excellent.
easy to use site not so easy to contact LA for guidance
Greedy Service Charges | Payment system is temperamental

The ability to save card details would be good - as long as it is secure and protected. The attachments section still needs work - like there is no option for a drawing just called 'elevations' or artists impressions....otherwise it is really getting a lot better....
Would like a 'button' added that you can press to send an automated 'reminder for payment' email to payment nominee
Hi morning very easy to use no problems thanks Phil

Like the layout of the website and the guide through the sections to complete. I am submitting a plan by post but am unaware at this stage where to send it, presumably this will become clear when the local council contacts me by email
No, your system is quite excellent, easy to follow and use

I had some difficulty uploading 2 documents as I got an unexplained error message. It would have been helpful if the form had told me that the documents had to be in PDF format before they could be uploaded.
Portal was easy to use once found, finding it in the first place wasn't easy.

Uploading information is painstaking.  Not allowing commas, the method and control is incredibly frustrating and time wasting.  The rigidity of inputting information makes the process unpleasant.  I tend to send direct to local authorities now and bypass the PP when I can
yes easy to use
This is so hard to navigate there is just so much information, i needed to do a change of use and i have really struggled as its not at all clear
Straightforward to use. Can't really ask for more.
still unable to upload BNG metric

While going through the application process, I got to the end (point 6) and had not encountered anywhere to upload documents. 

It would be useful to have a note at the beginning with instructions on how to add the necessary documents. Something like, "Once all the questions are answered, save the application and return to the home page on the portal, open the application from there and upload required documents."
every time I submit an application I tick a box that tells me it won't show me the information again, yet every time I have to tick the box again..
To make changes to our application was a relatively easy process.
Change the address finder to conventional post office address records database.  In rural areas, the address is often based on unclassified road name which is irrelevant.
The calculation of fees page does not cover all applications

It wasn't until further along in the application that it was clear photos and drawings could be attached which could have been stated earlier within the application but if I need to submit any further requests I'll know. Otherwise a straight forward and clear submittal form.
No, it went very smoothly
The Website is very confusing and most of the forms and information for the planning I wished to apply for were completely unnecessary
File management is difficult. There should be simpler ways to upload, delete and categorise them.
Please make the mapping work for large scale rural planning applications. A farm track of 1500m on a 50ha farm cannot be mapped using you system

When I enter the different nominee email addresses the subsequent emails come to my company's email address instead of direct to the nominee.
My email is info@jcrplanning.com. Thank you.
The process was  bit lengthy, but it was straightforward.

For a "lay" person using the planning portal for the first time, the process is challenging and, to a certain extent, requires a trial and error approach. Even though we had made careful preparation, the Design & Access Statement did not have a straight forward navigation; greater clarity of support with incremental instructions would have been helpful.

Not convinced that planning permission is necessary as proposed change will eliminate the need for LPG use and remove an eyesore (4 x bright red Calor gas bottles) and replace with a source of renewable energy (ASHP) which is encouraged by Gov.uk and for which a grant is currently available.
uploading documents is frustrating as it takes several attempts before it will upload.
Location plan function does not appear to work very well on rural lands. Could do with being a little more interactive and allowing 'freehand' drawing of boundaries
It would be good to bring back the autofill details where the applicants email address is put into the payment email section
Great
The requirement to pay an additional fees due to a condition imposed by the LA is outrageous
I thought that this was extremely complicated just to request a roof repair.   I appreciate I need consent to do this but there should be an easier way to request repair work!!!
Portal works very well, logical and does not glitch

I needed to submit a Heritage Statement - I sent it as an additional document - wonder if it would be helpful to add a checklist of documents required. I would not have included it had Denise Sheath not alerted me to do so.

I foumd it extremely difficult to get to the PP section for Tree works. It took me to the building works etc every time I tried to get to Garden/Tree works in conservation area. This should have its own box alongside the buildings section.
for the site location , need some update for the ease of location the boundary.
Mostly clear and easy, the issue is with the Validation and Checklist, tried to complete it with app, not easy, downloaded completed scanned and tried to load but file too large.
I could not see how to make a payment, without cancelling my original payment details and re entering. Very confusing
A fee of £85 is nothing short of extortion!

This is the first time I've ever done a planning application.  Whilst there is a lot of information to put together in support of an application, the validations/progress checks and prompts is really helpful for me navigating all the requirements.

the recent change in the payment nomination stage is a nuisance - there should be an option to tick 'applicant' so as to avoid entering them all over again

the document upload has an annoying glitch - once you upload a document, a green bar appears at the top of the screen to confirm this - that's fine, but then it hovvers there unecessarily and momentarily prevents full uploading of the next document - a little thing, but infuriating on the hundredth time
using it for the first time was quite difficult. it wasn't clear how the "Access Statement" and "CIL" should be provided and i could find the CIL proforma at all.
You have to concentrate but self explanatory.
There is still inconsistency with different planing authorities - you fill everything in on line required by the portal and the authorities try to tell you its non compliant
easy to use
good experience
Very time consuming given i only wanted to replace an existing fence adjacent to a listed building.
Really easy to use
recent planning portal modifications are fantastic. Easy to manage and their customer team is excellent .

Please re-design the section for uploading plans and documents. This took me hours to upload as I was not sure if I was to upload a file at a time adding description individually or simply upload as one file and simply list the ref codes in one block. This has been an issue for years and would definitely improve your service if a graphic designer could clarify the process for applicants. Also, I applaud your new block/ location service - much quicker and saves on map purchase cost. thanks Charles Karsenbarg
difficult for older clients impossible to up load sketch etc, have to post in
Quicker than Application to individual Councils, loading of Documents sometimes complex due to rejection of some Files, but generally satisfactory
It would be helpful to have more information about the different types of applications - and classification of fees. I am not a planner, and the language used is difficult to understand.
Using the portal was ok but trying to contact someone with any queries before completing has been difficult
Many or the questions are ambiguous, the form could be made simpler, e.g., when LDC has already been issued and minor changes are required, a seperate form would be usefull
Figuring out problems with non-accepted characters in file names when uploading multiple files is a real pain!
okay.
FOR AN APPLICATION FOR DISPLAY OF ADVERTISMENTS WHEN A BOX 'NO' FOR ILLUMINATION IS CHECKED IT DOES NOT APPEAR IN AS A 'NO' ANSWER IN THE APPLICATION RESULTING IN AN INVALID APPLICATION
the site location tool can be misleading and often excludes the buildings on site and thus resulted in this amendment
It was unclear how to fill in many of the sections. The wording is not user friendly, and some of the system did not work properly unfortunately
very easy to use.
ALL Very helpful KEEP IT UP. THANKS
So very very very expensive - it used to cost £35 for a household, now it's nearly ten times that and everyone wonders why we can't build anything.

The website is not working properly.  Loading a very small PDF had to be abandoned after an hour and I had to re-enter the drawing name & type details for other documents twice.  I reported it and had an initial response but an hour later still not had the contact I was promised 'shortly'.  I have abandoned it and will have to wait until the local authority tells me its invalid because they don't have all the documents.  A waste of everybody's time.
Your service fee is abhorrent!

I rarely use the Planning portal but it seems
well designed and very versatile

The Site and Location Plan creation tools are NOT user friendly.
For those who use the facility infrequently (perhaps once only) the tools are very difficult to use. To align a rectangle with an existing building is almost impossible.
A very good portal to use. Easy to understand and clear instructions.

I’ve already paid £192 to Wyre Forest District Council to find out I only need a change of use. It would be useful if local authorities signposted clients to the planning portal straight away as the service is informative and easy to navigate. This would save the client time and money, it’s taken three months so far. Business is tough enough without the unnecessary delays.
Easy to follow and submit for a simple application

Yes, there is an annoying list of documents designed to identify the type of document uploaded, which is very general and most of the time does not correspond to the document type. I wish this feature were removed, and that the document description were enough to identify it. It just slows down the application submission process
The application specifically said a plan was NOT required (no Tree Preservation Order), but in the next section it required a plan in order to proceed!!
not clear what drawings are required for a lawful development
It was great once I got going - took some time but it all worked really well, and Stephen Hill gave me some useful advice so I'm grateful for that.
My PC always created a password that was too long - changing it / editing it was not easy
I am replacing a failed roof which has seriously compromised my home and it has taken 6 months to get to this stage. Additionally, there is not a fee calculator for roof replacement.
It is hard to understand some of the terminology. Fees did not display for me so not sure if I have completed correctly
Very easy to use
Very good navigation, it was very clear what you needed me to do.  Good mechanism for uploading, categorising and describing the files.

Several Sections including the biodiversity and the land ownership didn't really offer what I thought were the appropriate responses for my application; an option to select "special circumstances" and offer a narrative explanation would have been useful.
It was reasonable. We are resubmitting a lapsed approved application. It would be good to have a box somewhere to say stuff like this.
Easy and convenient
Unbound joy.
Easy to use service.

I have been using the planning portal for a number of years as an agent.  After the most recent updates I seem to be unable to nominate my clients for the fee payment.  I input their email address and the system tells me that the nomination was sent, however I then get the email from the planning portal to my address asking for payment.  This has happened multiple times.  

I emailed the suggested address about this issue and it bounced back
Far too complicated for  tree maintenance .  A simple form would suffice but this is not possible as the portal has to cover planning applications from big buildings to minor tree work
not tailored to specific requirement (change of use) particularly those related to London planning authority
It is a useful tool, but time consuming for in experienced users
Great portal!  Would love to be able to upload multiple files at once.
Very Good
Easy system to use and follow.

Overall, the planning portal website is easy to navigate and use. The uploading of documents and drawings is made difficult by the file names requiring certain symbols to be removed (comma's for example). This would streamline the process for the better if this could be removed.
Straightforward
No

Difficulty with providing compliant location plan as property is some distance from the public highway and thus does not fit on an A4 plan. Some clearer information that you can submit your own location plan in these circumstances i.e. to a different scale would be helpful.

An obersation regarding the payment of fees not for this application but the last one I made, you should consider raising  your £1000 limit for card payments, due to the significant increase in planning application submission fees across the board
I submit around 60 applications a year. I don't see any advantage to having to pay for the Map. I am an architectural designer and the plans already have this information. It should be optional .
Very straight-forward and easy to use.
The link to the local document requirements on the documents section of the application leads to a 'page not found' error on the CCC website.

Hi,

I think that it would be a good idea if there would be a option to send the nominee to a few email addresses.

Thanks
Earlier notification of file upload Mb caps.
Generally satsified.

I am only submitting this because KCC instructed me to consult the planning authority before applying for a dropped kerb. The portal does not provide a clear option for dropped kerb enquiries, so I am using the Lawful Development route to obtain the required confirmation.
had to complete fields that were not required in my case to get form to submit.
the location map tool needs significant improving - on some sites it is impossible to draw the red & blue lines

When you click on submit, a message comes up with some info and it says "do not show again" but tells me again every time I submit a new planning application. Can that be changed so it doesn't show the same message again?

not easy to make changes if council request application amendments, especially if an additional fee is required.
the fee calculation page doesnt cover all types of applications.
I CAN NOT LOAD UP EXCEL DOCS AND CERTAIN OTHERS THAT HAVE PREVIOUSLY BEEN NAMED WITH A DASH OR A FORWARD SLASH IF THIS COULD BE SORTED THAT WOULD BE GREAT
The system was easy to follow and upload what was needed.
DONT KEEP INCREASING YOUR FEES AS LIFE IS ALREADY EXPENSIVE
Easy system to use, many thanks.
all good here

In the documents section, it is assumed that certain documents will be submitted. Not all of the mandatory fields are always relevant to an application, e.g. the replacement fence application that I had to submit because of of the fence line's location in a conservation area. In such instances, a block plan should be sufficient rather than existing and proposed elevations etc. Please review the fact that mandatory items cannot be switched off. A planning application needs to be proportionate to the proposals.

Please add a function where we can go to our applications on the Portal and find a link to the local authority website case file so that we can track applications via the Planning Portal. This would make your high fee for acceptable in my view.
Very good
I couldn't browse my computer to add a file but was able to drag and drop.
It seemed to go smoothly, language could be a little clearer and ins two with UI, but overall very good.

It's really good that its a standardised system across all authorities and that it sends to the appropriate council. 

It's a bit clunky, there's lots of scrolling which is a pain.
Very time consuming.
Very Straightforward. Best platform for Architects and Designers.

This is a very terrible service. The forms are not adapted to the works I'm doing, the questions are confusing. When I asked for a service I never received any timely answer. 

You are failling your citizens here: this means people incur costs to do important renovation work to live in their homes, have decent insulation, and do energy efficiency crucial works for the nation independence to fossil fuels. This is a large failure... It is extremely hard to get coherent and timely information. And the process where you are suppose to know how much you are charged is ridiculous. 
You shouldn't expect citizens to be experts at complex laws. 
You shouldn't add silly forms like the CIL for simple renovation projects...

And again, because the platform didn't manage to provide me with a fee estimation for a building control application, my application was delayed by another 3 weeks.

Similarly, some council officers told me this is common practice, and then encouraged me to bypass this portal, then other told me it is the only way.

If you have to have a single portal like that, it needs to work better.
Excellent service now it has been revamped.
I think your charges for using the service are extortionate. I shall be using this less and less from now on.
Uploading documents if it can be matched with local authority validity requirements it will be great
The questions need to have more information and examples

I don't like it trying to sell me a location plan and being a pushy about it
I don't like it advertising my competitors
Some of the multiple choice questions do not offer the correct answer, so I end up having to tick a wrong answer then e-mailing the planners to cover it off - we need more opportunity to say 'none of the above' and then enter appropriate info
The application is a nightmare. It has taken me 3 hours to complete it. But now I will look forward to your reply.
I found it very difficult to find the way of uploading a sketch plan to the site. It wasn't immediately obvious

I found the site very easy to follow and submit my Planning Application.
I would like the description of plans submitted to be altered to read Floor Plans and Elevations  or separate Floor Plans and Elevations of any description.
excellent
The application requires the name and address of the property owner with a UK postcode.  This is not possible if the owner lives abroad.
EASY TO FOLLOW WHEN YOU HAVE THE RIGHT DOCUMENTS TO HAND
The ability to duplicate Signage details on the same application would useful. (where multiple signs of the same size and spec are being proposed on the same application).
Unbelievably badly designed site with multiple repeat questions and often no clue as to how to progress.
Great team with amazing support from planning staff
Easy to navigate and upload documents

A bit more explination on the characters that can / cant be used in file names. I use [square brackets] and these caused an issue yet not in the description of bared / banned characters. Apart from the a ten start service. (please get reest of the goveremetn to run this smoothly)
shit
easy to use as a layperson
Unsure why the fees for householder applications jumped up so significantly without any warning - It would be good to get some feedback on this i fpossible
All good

Glitch with uploading documents after ticking document type box. We submit many applications with numerous documents and this continuously happens when ticking the document type box. The document type box clears after being checked and has to be recompleted.
Only at the end the consent to display advertisement did I have any confidence that it was correct, quite confusing.
It's confusing but workable. Ability to upload sketch plan was not clear early in the application.
The name entries continue to change after they have been saved on the form

Website application system is good and relatively straight forward to use. This is extremely positive. However, in terms of the actual planning system, I'm not sure that adequate consideration was made of my specifics leading up to the application. My small scale project fitted completely into "permitted development" save for its exact location relative to my dwellinghouse, chiefly because my (long) garden leads from the house towards the highway. So as a factor of just 10 metres of precise location, the project transforms from essentially "shed replacement" to "full-on ecological and biodiversity impact assessment" and from "DIY graph paper and pencil" to "Plans, location maps and supporting documentation." There appears to be little interim scaling of the system or indeed cost. Had I been able to place my small garden project just 10 metres further North (I cannot), I would have a) avoided the whole planning system, b) saved myself £347 and, importantly c) saved the planning department considerable time/effort. Given the significant effort, indeed within 500m of this site, to build large family houses, I would encourage that greater judgement and discretion should be made over insignificant domestic projects to free up the department for more primary and potentially controversial output.

I have used the Planning Portal for the past 20 years across a wide range of applications, and while it remains a widely recognised platform, there are several areas where the service could be improved.

The system itself is generally functional, but it can feel overly complex and not particularly user-friendly, especially for straightforward submissions. Navigation between sections is not always intuitive, and the process can be more time-consuming than necessary. In addition, the cost of submitting applications through the platform is relatively high, particularly when compared to the level of service provided.

Communication and transparency could also be improved. It is not always clear what stage an application is at without additional follow-up, and the overall user experience feels somewhat dated.

It is also worth noting that there are alternative platforms and submission routes available which offer a more streamlined experience and, in some cases, provide incentives or added value for using their services. This highlights that there is room for the Planning Portal to evolve and remain competitive.

Overall, while the Planning Portal serves its purpose, there is definite scope for improvement in terms of usability, value for money, and overall user experience.

I submitted a claim for CLEUD.  

1) Many questions are about "proposed" vs "existing" but in this case there is no proposed development, because all the changes took place 39 years ago! I had do some rather extreme logical gymnastics in order to guess/assume that (bizarrely) "proposed" in this case must actually mean "as currently existing for the past 39 years", and "existing" actually means "as it was more than 39 years ago before any change"

2) There was no structural work done, only a change of use, but the forms are not designed for this. 

3) At the stage of adding documents, it said only 1 out 2 steps were complete, but there was no indication of what was missing so I was unable to proceed.  At first I guessed that probably I am required to submit a "Site Plan" even though it is virtually the same as the "location Plan"  so I used the RequestaPlan service to create a Site Plan and added it. 

But it still said 1 out of 2 steps complete, and I was unable to proceed.  It seems that I had wasted nearly £10 at RequestaPlan.  My next guess was that perhaps I have to complete a CIL form.  (That seemed pointless really, as again this form is not designed for CLEUD cases where there is no development or structural work, only a change of use.) That did the trick.  But it should be clear - the software knows and imposes this CIL requirement.  So it should tell me!  I shouldn't have to guess .

Then, after literally hours of trying to complete the form, I was blocked at the last hurdle when it came to paying due a technical issue (NOT on my side). But, by that time, your support team had gone home so I was blocked and could not get assistance to proceed to complete the application. It has been a very exasperating and dispiriting experience. You need to make the process simpler for simple applications such as mine. You need to find a way to cut out the irrelevant questions. Also, it was annoying how the CIL form had to be downloaded and completed totally separately. You should somehow incorporate this in the main application because that was another item blocking the completion of my application form and it took time for me to understand that it was a separate item to download and complete. Finally, I also feel that for a simple application such as mine, the fee is excessively high. So, overall, it has not been a good experience and one that has left me utterly drained and demoralised.  I have made the application in good faith because I want to do things properly and I feel you need to make the process simple and easy for those who follow the rules and are trying to do things properly. If you make it a difficult and complicated process and then apply high fees on top, you just put people off. It is very important that everyone follows the rules and do things properly but, you must make it easy and cost effective otherwise you encourage people to avoid doing so.

I would like to provide feedback regarding the additional Planning Portal service charge.

As an applicant, I have completed the application form myself, prepared the supporting statement, uploaded all plans and supporting documents, and entered all required information directly into the system. Given that the applicant undertakes the majority of the administrative work, it is difficult to understand the justification for an additional service charge on top of the statutory planning fee.

The explanation that applications would otherwise need to be manually uploaded by the local authority is also difficult to reconcile with the fact that applicants are already providing all information in a digital format through the Planning Portal. From a customer perspective, the process appears to reduce rather than increase administrative work for both the applicant and the local authority.

Whilst I appreciate that there are costs associated with maintaining the Planning Portal system, greater transparency regarding how the service charge is calculated and the specific services it funds would be welcomed.

The Planning Portal provides a valuable service, but the additional charge can feel disproportionate, particularly for straightforward household applications where applicants have completed and submitted all documentation themselves.

Many application forms contain a large number of questions that are not relevant to the majority of proposals. This applies not only to householder applications but also to many full planning applications, such as change of use schemes, where applicants are still required to work through numerous sections that do not apply to the development.

It would be much more efficient if the application process included an initial screening or overview page where applicants could simply tick which topics are not applicable to their proposal. The system could then automatically hide or skip those sections, rather than requiring users to navigate through multiple pages and answer numerous "No" or "Not Applicable" questions.

This would make the process significantly quicker, reduce frustration, and help less experienced applicants and agents avoid spending unnecessary time trying to understand and complete irrelevant sections of the form.
